layers.name: Quantum Core
layers.description: Core quantum simulation primitives including Clifford+T gates, state-vector and tensor-network simulators, surface-code decoders, and error-correction infrastructure (ruQu, ruqu-core).

layers.name: Quantum Algorithms & Exotic
layers.description: High-level quantum algorithms (Grover, QAOA, VQE), exotic/experimental quantum routines, and WASM-targeted quantum runtimes (ruqu-algorithms, ruqu-exotic, ruqu-wasm).

layers.name: Hailo NPU
layers.description: Hailo NPU integration covering low-level HailoRT FFI bindings, single-device embedders, and multi-device cluster pooling (hailort-sys, ruvector-hailo, ruvector-hailo-cluster).

layers.name: Sparse Inference
layers.description: Mixed-precision sparse inference engine with precision lanes, activation predictors, GGUF loaders, and WASM bindings (ruvector-sparse-inference, ruvector-sparse-inference-wasm).

layers.name: FPGA + mmWave
layers.description: FPGA transformer accelerator backends, WASM-targeted FPGA shims, and mmWave radar packet parsing pipelines (ruvector-fpga-transformer, ruvector-fpga-transformer-wasm, ruvector-mmwave).

tour.title: Quantum + HW accelerator cluster: workspace overview
tour.description: Slice 10c bundles RuVector's quantum simulation stack with three hardware-accelerator paths (Hailo NPU, FPGA transformer, mmWave radar) and the sparse-inference engine that ties them to model serving. Start at the per-crate READMEs to anchor vocabulary across ruqu-core (state-vector sim + decoders), ruqu-algorithms (Grover/QAOA/VQE), ruqu-exotic (interference/swarm), the Hailo cluster coordinator, the FPGA transformer backends, and the sparse inference engine that feeds them.

tour.title: Quantum Core: ruqu-core simulator, gates, decoders, providers
tour.description: ruqu-core is the foundation: a pure-Rust state-vector simulator with Clifford+T support, surface-code decoders (UnionFind, sub-polynomial, hierarchical-tiled), Kraus-based noise models, transpilation, and a HardwareProvider abstraction that lets the same circuit target a local sim, IBM Quantum, or IonQ. The Simulator/QuantumState pair holds the live wavefunction while SurfaceCodeDecoder + decomposition::stoer_wagner_mincut drive error correction. Everything downstream in this slice consumes the Gate/QuantumCircuit/Hamiltonian types declared here.

tour.title: Quantum Algorithms + ruQu adaptive fabric
tour.description: Building on ruqu-core's primitives, ruqu-algorithms ships the canonical quantum recipes (Grover search with optimal iteration count, QAOA MaxCut, VQE hardware-efficient ansatz, and a surface-code QEC harness). ruQu wraps them in a 'classical nervous system' for live quantum hardware: a QuantumFabric/PatchGraph that streams syndromes through MWPMDecoder/StreamingDecoder, with AdaptiveThresholds + DriftDetector retuning the pipeline as device noise wanders.

tour.title: Exotic quantum-inspired reasoning + WASM bindings
tour.description: ruqu-exotic explores speculative reasoning kernels (interference, collapse, decay, swarm, reversible memory) used as discovery primitives, with cross-pipeline tests that combine decoherence, swarm reasoning, and QEC. ruqu-wasm then exports the deterministic chunks of ruqu-core/algorithms to the browser via wasm-bindgen, exposing WasmQuantumCircuit, Grover, and a QAOA-MaxCut entry point so JS callers can run small circuits client-side.

tour.title: Hailo NPU: single-device embedder up through the multi-Pi cluster
tour.description: The Hailo path layers cleanly: hailort-sys provides bindgen FFI to libhailort behind a `hailo` feature gate; ruvector-hailo's HailoEmbedder auto-selects between the native HEF pipeline (HefBackend) and a CPU fallback. ruvector-hailo-cluster turns that into a fleet: HailoClusterEmbedder orchestrates Discovery (Static/File/Tailscale), routes through a P2cPool (power-of-two-choices over an EmbeddingTransport), and quorum-checks model fingerprints across workers before serving embeddings.

tour.title: Sparse Inference: precision lanes, predictor, GGUF, model runners
tour.description: The sparse-inference crate is where models actually run. PrecisionLane enumerates 3/5/7-bit + FP lanes (with HardwareTarget gating SIMD/NPU/CPU choice), the LowRankPredictor forecasts which FFN neurons activate so SparseInferenceEngine can skip the rest, GgufParser loads quantized weights from disk, and three concrete ModelRunner impls (LlamaModel, LFM2Model, BertModel) plug into the unified Backend trait (CPU/NPU/WASM). SparseEmbeddingProvider wires the whole stack into RuVector's EmbeddingProvider interface.

tour.title: FPGA Transformer: artifacts, backends, FFI
tour.description: ruvector-fpga-transformer packages a transformer as a signed ModelArtifact (manifest + weights + test vectors, Ed25519-verified) and runs it through one of four interchangeable TransformerBackends: FpgaDaemonBackend (out-of-process over a socket), FpgaPcieBackend (direct DMA ring buffer + slot tracking), NativeSimBackend (software for CI), and WasmSimBackend. The top-level Engine binds backend + DefaultCoherenceGate + witness log, and the FFI module exposes both a C ABI (FpgaEngine/FpgaResult) and a wasm-bindgen surface for native and browser callers.

tour.title: mmWave Radar: frame parser + cluster bridge
tour.description: Finishing the slice, the mmwave crate is a streaming parser for Seeed MR60BHA1/MR60FDA1 radar frames that decodes presence, heart, and breath signals. The Hailo cluster ships a dedicated mmwave-bridge binary that turns those parsed radar features into embedding requests against the cluster from Step 5, closing the loop from sensor to NPU-served embedding. Integration tests cover both plain and TLS bridge flows.
